content: git init requirements for integration tests: agentmap requires a git repo (reads git metadata for staleness, caching). Tests call gitInit(dir) which runs git init, sets user.email/name, disables gpgsign, sets hooksPath=/dev/null. CI pitfalls: (1) git must be installed — GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest has git pre-installed; (2) git config --global safe.directory — Git 2.35.2+ added ownership checks; in CI the temp dir is owned by the runner user so this is not an issue, but running tests as root (e.g. in Docker containers without user) will fail with 'dubious ownership' unless safe.directory is set; (3) git commit requires author identity — helpers.mjs sets this per-repo which is correct; global git config in CI may be minimal but per-repo config overrides it; (4) --no-verify flag on commit prevents hook interference correctly.
